title: Longitudinal O*NET Archive Analysis
text: -vintage comparisons easy case. next example uses bundled 2010-vintage 2019-vintage fixtures. bridge intentionally tiny, shows real problem: 2010 occupation can split one 2019 occupation.
code: cross_archives <- c(   `24.3` = example_archive("24.3"),   `25.1` = example_archive("25.1") ) cross_dates <- c(`24.3` = "2020-08-01", `25.1` = "2020-11-01")  cross_panel <- onet_panel(   "Abilities",   versions = c("24.3", "25.1"),   scale = "IM",   archives = cross_archives,   release_dates = cross_dates )  bridge_2010_2019 <- tibble::tibble(   from_vintage = "2010",   to_vintage = "2019",   from_onet_soc_code = c("15-1132.00", "15-1132.00", "29-1141.00"),   to_onet_soc_code = c("15-1252.00", "15-1253.00", "29-1141.00"),   map_type = c("split", "split", "one_to_one"),   crosswalk_weight = c(0.5, 0.5, 1) )  cross_changes <- onet_panel_reconcile(cross_panel, bridge_2010_2019)  cross_changes |>   select(     from_onet_soc_code,     to_onet_soc_code,     element_name,     change_type,     crosswalk_uncertain,     transition_data,     safely_comparable   ) |>   knitr::kable(digits = 3, align = "l") plot_changes <- cross_changes |>   filter(!is.na(from_value), !is.na(to_value)) |>   mutate(     comparison = paste(to_onet_soc_code, element_name, sep = ": "),     comparability = if_else(safely_comparable, "Safe", "Not safe")   )  ggplot2::ggplot(plot_changes, ggplot2::aes(y = comparison)) +   ggplot2::geom_segment(     ggplot2::aes(       x = from_value,       xend = to_value,       yend = comparison,       color = comparability     ),     linewidth = 1.1   ) +   ggplot2::geom_point(     ggplot2::aes(x = from_value),     color = onet2r_colors[["slate"]],     size = 2.8   ) +   ggplot2::geom_point(     ggplot2::aes(x = to_value, color = comparability),     size = 3.2   ) +   onet2r_discrete_color(name = "Comparability") +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "A Cross-Vintage Difference Is Not Automatically Comparable",     subtitle = "Start and end points come from the bundled archive fixtures.",     x = "Importance rating",     y = NULL   ) +   onet2r_theme()

what: Compare OEWS with a PUMS-Style Weight Panel
title: Adding OEWS Wage and Employment Context
text: OEWS right default official occupation employment wage context. PUMS-derived weights useful target population custom sample, geography, demographic cell. PUMS-style panel cells, pick one cell aggregating. real ACS PUMS, filter employment universe building weights. common starting point employed civilians, often ESR %% c(1, 2) age 16 older. SOCP SOC field. OCCP Census occupation recode needs separate crosswalk can used O*NET OEWS. Neither source always better. OEWS gives official labor market estimates; PUMS gives custom cells. package records weight source, year, taxonomy, coverage reader can see changed.
code: pums <- tibble::tibble(   SOCP = c("151252", "151252", "291141", "291141", "111011"),   PWGTP = c(120, 80, 90, 110, 20),   sex = c("F", "M", "F", "M", "F") )  pums_weights <- onet_weight_panel_pums(   pums,   year = 2022,   group = "sex" )  pums_weights |>   knitr::kable(digits = 3, align = "l") pums <- tidycensus::get_pums(   variables = c("SOCP", "PWGTP", "ESR", "AGEP"),   state = "WA",   year = 2023,   survey = "acs1" ) |>   filter(ESR %in% c("1", "2"), AGEP >= 16)  pums_weights <- onet_weight_panel_pums(pums, year = 2023) oral_pums_f <- onet_measure_aggregate(   oral_scores,   pums_weights,   measure_id = "oral_comprehension_fixture",   cell = list(sex = "F") )  tibble::tibble(   weight_source = c("OEWS national", "PUMS-style F cell"),   aggregate = c(oral_oews$aggregate, oral_pums_f$aggregate),   coverage = c(     oral_oews$employment_coverage_share,     oral_pums_f$employment_coverage_share   ) ) |>   knitr::kable(digits = 3, align = "l")

what: Build the Measure from Task Files
title: Stress-Testing a User-Supplied Exposure Measure
code: tasks_243 <- onet_archive_read(   "24.3",   "Task Statements",   path = archive_path("24.3"),   release_date = "2020-08-01" ) ratings_243 <- onet_archive_read(   "24.3",   "Task Ratings",   path = archive_path("24.3"),   release_date = "2020-08-01" ) tasks_251 <- onet_archive_read(   "25.1",   "Task Statements",   path = archive_path("25.1"),   release_date = "2020-11-01" ) ratings_251 <- onet_archive_read(   "25.1",   "Task Ratings",   path = archive_path("25.1"),   release_date = "2020-11-01" )  task_scores <- tibble::tibble(   task_id = c("1101", "1201", "1202", "2101"),   score = c(0.75, 0.80, 0.55, 0.25) )  measure <- onet_measure(   task_scores,   key = "task_id",   score = "score",   key_type = "task",   universe = unique(c(tasks_243$task_id, tasks_251$task_id)),   measure_id = "stylized_exposure",   release_version = "multi_release_fixture" )  onet_coverage(measure) |>   knitr::kable(digits = 3, align = "l")

title: Stress-Testing a User-Supplied Exposure Measure
code: bridge_2010_2019 <- tibble::tibble(   from_vintage = "mixed_fixture",   to_vintage = "2018 SOC",   from_onet_soc_code = c(     "15-1132.00",     "15-1132.00",     "15-1252.00",     "15-1253.00",     "29-1141.00"   ),   to_onet_soc_code = c(     "15-1252.00",     "15-1253.00",     "15-1252.00",     "15-1253.00",     "29-1141.00"   ),   map_type = c("split", "split", "one_to_one", "one_to_one", "one_to_one"),   crosswalk_weight = c(0.5, 0.5, 1, 1, 1) )  sensitivity <- onet_measure_sensitivity(   measure,   weight_panels = list(oews = oews_weights, pums = pums_weights),   bridges = list(reference_bridge = bridge_2010_2019),   task_ratings = list(`2010-vintage 24.3` = ratings_243, `2019-vintage 25.1` = ratings_251),   task_metadata = list(`2010-vintage 24.3` = tasks_243, `2019-vintage 25.1` = tasks_251),   include_supplemental = c(FALSE, TRUE) )  sensitivity |>   select(     scenario,     task_release,     soc_vintage,     weight_panel,     include_supplemental,     aggregate,     employment_coverage_share,     movement,     movement_percent   ) |>   knitr::kable(digits = 3, align = "l") plot_sensitivity <- sensitivity |>   mutate(     task_rule = if_else(include_supplemental, "core + supplemental", "core only"),     plot_label = paste0(task_release, "  |  ", weight_panel, "  |  ", task_rule)   )  ggplot2::ggplot(plot_sensitivity, ggplot2::aes(   x = aggregate,   y = stats::reorder(plot_label, aggregate),   color = weight_panel,   shape = task_rule )) +   ggplot2::geom_vline(     xintercept = plot_sensitivity$baseline_aggregate[[1]],     color = onet2r_colors[["slate"]],     linetype = "dashed"   ) +   ggplot2::geom_point(size = 3.6) +   ggplot2::scale_color_manual(     values = c(oews = onet2r_colors[["teal"]], pums = onet2r_colors[["amber"]]),     name = "Weight source"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_shape_manual(     values = c("core only" = 16, "core + supplemental" = 17),     name = "Task rule"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(     expand = ggplot2::expansion(mult = c(0.08, 0.12))   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "Sensitivity to task release, weights, and task handling",     subtitle = "Dashed line marks the first scenario in the grid.",     x = "Aggregate score",     y = NULL   ) +   onet2r_theme() +   ggplot2::theme(legend.box = "vertical", legend.margin = ggplot2::margin())

title: Within-Versus-Between Decomposition
text: comparability flag comes onet_panel_reconcile(), hand-labeled example table. Rows cross split transition data kept accounting get treated clean within-occupation change.  component rows total_change sum total change.
code: archive_base <- onet2r_inst_path("extdata", "onet-mini") cross_panel <- onet_panel(   "Abilities",   versions = c("24.3", "25.1"),   scale = "IM",   archives = c(     `24.3` = file.path(archive_base, "db_24_3_text"),     `25.1` = file.path(archive_base, "db_25_1_text")   ),   release_dates = c(`24.3` = "2020-08-01", `25.1` = "2020-11-01") )  bridge <- tibble::tibble(   from_vintage = "2010",   to_vintage = "2019",   from_onet_soc_code = c("15-1132.00", "15-1132.00", "29-1141.00"),   to_onet_soc_code = c("15-1252.00", "15-1253.00", "29-1141.00"),   map_type = c("split", "split", "one_to_one"),   crosswalk_weight = c(0.5, 0.5, 1) )  ability_changes <- onet_panel_reconcile(cross_panel, bridge) score_changes <- ability_changes |>   filter(element_id == "1.A.1.a.1", !is.na(from_value), !is.na(to_value)) |>   mutate(reference_soc_code = sub("\\.\\d{2}$", "", to_onet_soc_code))  from_scores <- score_changes |>   select(     reference_soc_code,     measure_score = from_value,     safely_comparable   ) to_scores <- score_changes |>   select(     reference_soc_code,     measure_score = to_value,     safely_comparable   )  from_weights <- tibble::tibble(   reference_soc_code = c("15-1252", "15-1253", "29-1141"),   employment = c(120, 80, 200) ) to_weights <- tibble::tibble(   reference_soc_code = c("15-1252", "15-1253", "29-1141"),   employment = c(170, 110, 160) )  from_scores |>   knitr::kable(digits = 3, align = "l") to_scores |>   knitr::kable(digits = 3, align = "l") decomp <- onet_decompose_change(   from_scores,   to_scores,   from_weights,   to_weights )  decomp |>   select(component, value) |>   knitr::kable(digits = 3, align = "l") onet_coverage(decomp) |>   knitr::kable(digits = 3, align = "l") components <- decomp |>   filter(component != "total_change") |>   mutate(     label = tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", component)),     label = factor(label, levels = rev(label)),     direction = if_else(value >= 0, "Raises the aggregate", "Lowers the aggregate")   )  ggplot2::ggplot(components, ggplot2::aes(x = value, y = label, fill = direction)) +   ggplot2::geom_col(width = 0.62) +   ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = onet2r_colors[["slate"]]) +   ggplot2::geom_text(     ggplot2::aes(       label = sprintf("%+.3f", value),       hjust = if_else(value >= 0, -0.15, 1.15)     ),     size = 3.5,     color = onet2r_colors[["ink"]]   ) +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c(       "Raises the aggregate" = onet2r_colors[["teal"]],       "Lowers the aggregate" = onet2r_colors[["rose"]]     )   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(     expand = ggplot2::expansion(mult = c(0.16, 0.16))   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "Where did the aggregate change come from?",     subtitle = "Each piece's contribution to the total change.",     x = "Contribution to total change",     y = NULL,     fill = NULL   ) +   onet2r_theme() decomp |>   summarise(     component_sum = sum(value[component != "total_change"]),     total_change = value[component == "total_change"],     difference = component_sum - total_change   ) |>   knitr::kable(digits = 3, align = "l")

Archived O*NET data: onet_releases(), onet_archive_download(), onet_archive_read(), onet_panel(), onet_panel_reconcile(). Wage employment context: onet_oews_national(), onet_weight_panel_oews(), onet_weight_panel_pums(). title: Work with O*NET Web Services, archives, and weights from R
text: ONETr (CRAN) wraps retired O*NET Web Services v1 XML API username/password auth; predates v2 API package targets. blscrapeR / blsAPI wrap BLS time-series API, serve OEWS research flat files onet2r ingests; complementary, overlapping. tidycensus / ipumsr right tools fetching ACS PUMS microdata; onet2r deliberately Census fetcher consumes output employment weights. R Python tool assembles longitudinal panels historical O*NET database releases; machinery unique onet2r.

title: Measure O*NET Content Change Between Releases — onet_content_change
text: Computes task-set rating content-change metrics occupation O*NET releases. single source content metrics longitudinal work, robustness measures silently diverge. seam-aware: comparisons cross v21.0 Task Relevance retirement v25.1 SOC-2010 SOC-2018 change flagged taxonomy churn counted content churn.

title: Measure O*NET Content Change Between Releases — onet_content_change
text: panel tibble onet_panel() (Task Ratings panel) schema. Must contain onet_soc_code, release_version, release_date, soc_vintage, data_value, item key column. scale_id required scale NULL. scale Optional scale id used rating metrics item membership, example "IM" Importance. Use NULL keep every scale. item Column identifies content item within occupation. Defaults "task_id". Item membership drives set metrics. min_importance Optional numeric floor applied data_value selected scale. Items floor, missing data_value, dropped set membership computed. Use apply Importance filter removes post-v21.0 Task Relevance artifact. , Optional release versions selecting single comparison. supplied pair returned; otherwise every adjacent release pair compared release-date order. seams Optional data frame seam_type seam_date columns overrides default Task-Ratings seam table returned onet_known_seams(). Use non-Task-Ratings inputs Work Activities, Work Context, Abilities, v21.0 Task Relevance scale seam apply. Supply empty table disable date-based seams entirely. NULL keeps default table, Task Ratings output unchanged. Cross-vintage SOC seams always detected soc_vintage regardless table.

title: Measure O*NET Content Change Between Releases — onet_content_change
text: tibble one row per occupation release pair: n_from, n_to, n_added, n_dropped, n_retained; jaccard (set similarity, retained share item union); churn_rate (1 - jaccard); rating_delta_l2 (Euclidean norm data_value changes retained items); cosine (cosine similarity data_value vectors item union, missing items filled zero); seam flags seam, seam_type, safely_comparable.

title: Measure O*NET Content Change Between Releases — onet_content_change
text: Set metrics compare item keys occupation carries release. rating_delta_l2 uses retained items, measures rating drift among tasks persist; cosine uses union zero fill, responds membership turnover rating change. Seam-crossing pairs still receive metrics marked safely_comparable = FALSE; include clearly labeled seam-inclusive row. Occupations matched onet_soc_code, cross-taxonomy pairs generally share codes bridged onet_crosswalk_bridge() interpretation.

title: Measure O*NET Content Change Between Releases — onet_content_change
code: panel <- tibble::tibble(   release_version = c(rep("22.1", 3), rep("23.1", 3)),   release_date = c(rep(as.Date("2017-10-01"), 3), rep(as.Date("2018-11-01"), 3)),   soc_vintage = "2010",   onet_soc_code = "15-1132.00",   task_id = c("1", "2", "3", "1", "2", "4"),   scale_id = "IM",   data_value = c(4.0, 3.5, 2.0, 4.5, 3.5, 3.0) ) onet_content_change(panel) #> # A tibble: 1 × 21 #>   onet_soc_code soc_code title from_release to_release from_release_date #>   <chr>         <chr>    <chr> <chr>        <chr>      <date>            #> 1 15-1132.00    15-1132  NA    22.1         23.1       2017-10-01        #> # ℹ 15 more variables: to_release_date <date>, soc_vintage_from <fct>, #> #   soc_vintage_to <fct>, n_from <int>, n_to <int>, n_added <int>, #> #   n_dropped <int>, n_retained <int>, jaccard <dbl>, churn_rate <dbl>, #> #   rating_delta_l2 <dbl>, cosine <dbl>, seam <lgl>, seam_type <chr>, #> #   safely_comparable <lgl>

code: # Offline: broadcast a small local extract onto a tiny panel. extract <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") utils::write.csv(   data.frame(     `O*NET-SOC Code` = c("15-1252.00", "29-1141.00"),     Title = c("Software Developers", "Registered Nurses"),     human_rating_beta = c(0.63, 0.14),     check.names = FALSE   ),   extract,   row.names = FALSE ) panel <- tibble::tibble(   onet_soc_code = rep(c("15-1252.00", "29-1141.00"), each = 2),   task_id = c("1", "2", "3", "4") ) measure <- onet_import_eloundou(panel, path = extract) measure$data #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>   onet_soc_code task_id human_rating_beta measure_key measure_score #>   <chr>         <chr>   <chr>             <chr>               <dbl> #> 1 15-1252.00    1       0.63              1                    0.63 #> 2 15-1252.00    2       0.63              2                    0.63 #> 3 29-1141.00    3       0.14              3                    0.14 #> 4 29-1141.00    4       0.14              4                    0.14  # Online: download the published occupation-level table. if (interactive()) {   exposure <- onet_import_eloundou(panel)   head(exposure$data) }

title: Create a Bring-Your-Own O*NET Measure — onet_measure
text: Two construction paths available. default path validates table key score columns already built. convenience path, selected passing items agg, builds task-grain measure one line Task Ratings style panel: restricts panel caller's target items (agg = "targeted") keeps every item (agg = "aggregate"), selects one rating row per task scale (default Importance, "IM"), keys result item (default "task_id"). result exactly measure default path returns subset, thin convenience wrapper, new estimator. Roll occupations onet_task_to_occupation() onet_measure_aggregate(). target items, package, carry substantive judgement. single items vector expresses one target set, switch builds targeted composite routine-task composite. difference index like routine-task intensity, subtracts abstract manual composites routine composite, composed separately two measures.

code: scores <- tibble::tibble(   onet_soc_code = c("15-1252.00", "29-1141.00"),   score = c(0.7, 0.2) ) universe <- c("15-1252.00", "29-1141.00", "11-1011.00") measure <- onet_measure(scores, "onet_soc_code", "score", universe = universe) onet_measure_coverage(measure) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   key_type   n_input n_universe n_matched coverage_share employment_coverage_s…¹ #>   <chr>        <int>      <int>     <int>          <dbl>                   <dbl> #> 1 occupation       2          3         2          0.667                      NA #> # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​employment_coverage_share  # Convenience path: build a task-grain targeted measure from a panel in one # line, ready to roll up with onet_task_to_occupation(). panel <- tibble::tibble(   onet_soc_code = rep(c("15-1252.00", "29-1141.00"), each = 3),   task_id = c("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6"),   scale_id = "IM",   data_value = c(4.5, 3.0, 2.0, 1.0, 4.0, 3.0) ) targeted <- onet_measure(panel, items = c("1", "5"), agg = "targeted") targeted$data #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   onet_soc_code task_id scale_id data_value measure_key measure_score #>   <chr>         <chr>   <chr>         <dbl> <chr>               <dbl> #> 1 15-1252.00    1       IM              4.5 1                     4.5 #> 2 29-1141.00    5       IM              4   5                     4

Suppression top-coding flagged separately (see *_topcoded suppression indicator columns). *_prse columns percent relative standard errors corresponding estimates.

title: Download OEWS Estimates — onet_oews
text: BLS may reject automated ZIP downloads HTTP 403 even URL downloads browser. onet_oews() handles case three steps: first checks package cache, looks matching ZIP Downloads folder, interactive sessions opens official BLS URL browser waits downloaded file appear. Set options(onet2r.oews_download_dir = "path//downloads") search different manual-download folder. Set options(onet2r.oews_browser_fallback = FALSE) disable opening browser. Set options(onet2r.oews_browser_wait = 120) control many seconds onet_oews() waits browser download. can always pass ZIP path directly path; stable fallback BLS changes OEWS URLs file names.

text: Restructures longitudinal O*NET panel task resurvey-cycle frame used study occupations actually re-rated. title: Assemble a Task by Resurvey-Cycle Panel — onet_resurvey_panel
text: panel tibble onet_panel() (Task Ratings panel) schema. Must contain onet_soc_code, release_version, release_date, soc_vintage, source_date, domain_source, data_value, item key column. scale_id required scale NULL. scale Optional scale id filter single rating, example "IM" Importance. Filtering keeps one row per occupation, item, release. Use NULL keep every scale. item Column identifies content item within occupation. Defaults "task_id". survey_sources Character vector domain_source values count incumbent survey rotation. Defaults Incumbent Occupational Expert. Matching case-insensitive substring. min_importance Optional numeric floor applied data_value (selected scale). Rows floor, missing data_value, dropped. Use reproduce Importance filter removes post-v21.0 Task Relevance artifact. seams Optional data frame seam_type seam_date columns overrides default Task-Ratings seam table returned onet_known_seams(). Use non-Task-Ratings inputs Work Activities, Work Context, Abilities, v21.0 Task Relevance scale seam apply. row date-based seam seam_date falls interval release prior release. Supply empty table disable date-based seams entirely. NULL keeps default table, Task Ratings output unchanged. Cross-vintage SOC seams always detected soc_vintage regardless table.

title: Assemble a Task by Resurvey-Cycle Panel — onet_resurvey_panel
text: tibble one row per occupation, item, release. Alongside identifier rating columns carries resurvey clock: survey_source (survey, analyst, , unknown), occ_survey_date (occupation survey clock, latest survey source_date release), prev_survey_date, prev_release_version, resurvey_event (survey clock advanced versus occupation's prior release within taxonomy era), cycle_index (cumulative resurvey count), age_resolved (years staleness resurvey resolved), incoming seam flags seam_in seam_type.

title: Assemble a Task by Resurvey-Cycle Panel — onet_resurvey_panel
text: occupation survey clock newest source_date among rows whose domain_source survey source; Analyst - Transition carry-forward rows excluded clock. Resurvey events cycle indices computed within soc_vintage era v25.1 SOC seam never registers resurvey. Pair frame onet_condition_on_resurvey() obtain -risk set selection reasons.

text: onet_weight_panel_pums() expects ACS PUMS records already filtered employment universe used analysis. employment weights, common ACS starting point employed civilians, ESR %% c(1, 2), often restricted age 16 older. Use SOCP occupation field. OCCP Census occupation recode, SOC field. ACS SOCP can include aggregate codes trailing X characters. rows matched directly O*NET OEWS SOC codes, dropped warning unless caller supplies crosswalk maps valid reference SOC codes. Replicate-weight standard errors use sqrt((4 / R) * sum((theta_r - theta)^2)). ACS PUMS, pass full 80 PWGTP1 PWGTP80 columns. Passing smaller subset produces warning resulting standard error survey-valid. year argument means 1-year ACS PUMS data year. 5-year PUMS files pool responses across vintages can mix SOCP code versions within one file; SOC-vintage labeling reliable . Prefer 1-year files weight panels, resolve codes explicitly crosswalk.

text: onet_resurvey_panel() restructures Task Ratings panel task resurvey-cycle frame keyed incumbent-survey source_date, exposing occupation survey clock, resurvey events, cycle index, seam flags. onet_condition_on_resurvey() labels row selection_reason (resurveyed, unrevisited, taxonomy_seam, suppressed) at_risk flag, resurvey denominator change estimation. v25.1 SOC carry-forward treated taxonomy seam, resurvey. onet_content_change() single, seam-aware source content metrics releases: n_added, n_dropped, n_retained, jaccard, churn_rate, rating_delta_l2, cosine. Pairs crossing v21.0 v25.1 seam flagged safely_comparable = FALSE taxonomy churn counted content churn. onet_import_eloundou() ingests occupation-level GPT-exposure table Eloundou et al. (2023), “GPTs GPTs”, broadcasts onto tasks caller-supplied panel, returning task-grain onet_measure() object keyed (occupation, task) feeds onet_task_to_occupation(). Every task inherits occupation’s published score; structurally blind broadcast aggregate construction task-aware measures contrasted . thin adapter selects score column records provenance without transforming published values. MIT-licensed data never bundled; supply local path download url. onet_import_felten_aioe() ingests AI Occupational Exposure (AIOE) scores Felten, Raj, Seamans (2021) broadcasts onto panel tasks way, joining 6-digit SOC code, following download-, -transform adapter pattern. onet_measure() gains items = / agg = convenience path: given Task Ratings style panel builds task-grain measure one line. agg = "targeted" restricts panel set target task ids; agg = "aggregate" keeps every task. Either way selects one rating row per task scale (default Importance) keys task id, result exactly measure default data / key / score path returns subset, ready onet_task_to_occupation(). existing data / key / score / key_type path unchanged. onet_resurvey_panel() onet_content_change() gain optional seams = argument override default Task-Ratings-scoped seam table. default (NULL) reproduces existing output exactly; supplying table lets non-Task-Ratings inputs Work Activities, Work Context, Abilities drop v21.0 Task Relevance scale seam apply . Cross-vintage SOC seams still detected soc_vintage regardless.

title: onet2r 0.4.2
text: Archive tables unrecognized layouts now abort instead returning -NA panels. OEWS annual/hourly flags parsed logicals wage-field semantics documented. onet_change_summary() gains crosswalk-weighted counts (n_weighted, share_weighted). onet_measure_aggregate() reports large unmatched weight-panel employment shares. onet_releases() memoised per session, reports archive format, documents text-archive floor (20.1, October 2015). Reconciliation now reports occupations missing crosswalk bridge (coverage_status values "unmapped_source" / "unmapped_target").

title: onet2r 0.4.1
text: Rebuilt README article figures shared ggplot2 style pkgdown examples render readable, dark-mode-safe output instead base graphics. Replaced broad console table dumps README vignettes bounded knitr::kable() tables actual onet2r function results. Expanded longitudinal archive, OEWS, sensitivity, decomposition articles practical cross-vintage examples using packaged fixtures. Added pkgdown callouts, branded favicon colors, citation metadata, lifecycle badge documentation, release notes v0.4.1 polish pass. Strengthened fixture tests task rating scale handling multi-cell aggregation guards. onet_cache_clear() can clear API responses, O*NET archives, O*NET crosswalks, OEWS downloads, full package cache. onet_change_summary() now reports full change-type distribution within summary group instead modal change type. onet_decompose_change() now honors comparability flags supplied either period treats missing comparability safe within-change attribution. onet_measure_aggregate() now collapses multiple O*NET detail occupations within reference SOC applying employment weights, preventing inflated aggregates coverage shares 100 percent. onet_task_to_occupation() now carries measure id release metadata occupation-level rollups onet_measure_aggregate() provenance remains intact. onet_oews() now uses corrected BLS metro industry file slugs, writes OEWS downloads atomically, preserves top-coded suppressed wage flags, documents OEWS special value markers. onet_weight_panel_oews() now filters OEWS hierarchy rows detailed occupations computing weight shares. onet_weight_panel_pums() now warns unfiltered ACS employment universes, drops unweightable SOCP aggregate codes, warns missing person weights, warns replicate standard errors requested without full replicate set.

title: onet2r 0.4.0
text: onet_coverage() onet_provenance() expose coverage provenance metadata durable accessors instead requiring users inspect attributes. onet_decompose_change() now returns coverage metadata accessor-readable list-column. onet_join_oews(), onet_pums_employment_weights(), onet_weighted_summary() now emit lifecycle guidance toward vintage-aware onet_weight_panel_oews(), onet_weight_panel_pums(), onet_measure_aggregate() workflow. onet_measure_aggregate() now requires single weight year one optional cell, explicit year cell filters multi-period grouped panels. onet_measure_sensitivity() runs user-supplied measure across alternative bridges, weight panels, task-handling choices, reports baseline movement provenance. Added cross-vintage task-rating fixtures tests articles can demonstrate 2010--2019 O*NET-SOC behavior without network access.

title: onet2r 0.3.0
text: Added CI, README.Rmd, reproducible package logo source cleaner release maintenance. onet_archive_read() now preserves task DWA native fields reading O*NET Task Statements, Task Ratings, Tasks DWAs, 30.3 GWAs IWAs DWAs file. onet_crosswalk_bridge() now keeps 8-digit O*NET-SOC detail codes native bridge grain includes derived 6-digit SOC columns employment joins. onet_decompose_change() decomposes aggregate change within, , interaction, unclassifiable components sum--total check. onet_measure(), onet_task_to_occupation(), onet_measure_aggregate(), onet_robustness_diagnostic() add bring---measure validation, task--occupation aggregation, employment-weighted aggregation, provenance, plumbing-sensitivity checks. onet_panel_reconcile() now classifies transition rows, suppressed rows, new rows, dropped rows explicitly instead counting ordinary matched updates. onet_reference_soc_resolve(), onet_weight_panel_oews(), onet_weight_panel_pums() create reference-SOC mappings normalized employment-weight panels OEWS PUMS workflows.

title: onet2r 0.2.0
text: Added longitudinal O*NET background article verified reference file help users understand archive panels, taxonomy changes, comparability limits interpreting release--release changes. onet_archive_download() downloads text-format O*NET database archives release version reproducible local analysis. onet_archive_read() reads descriptor tables downloaded archives normalized long panel schema now accepts local ZIP files extracted archive directories offline workflows. onet_change_summary() summarizes reconciled O*NET changes overall SOC job family. onet_crosswalk_bridge() builds SOC taxonomy bridges across supported O*NET vintages labels one--one, split, merge mappings. onet_panel() assembles descriptor tables across multiple O*NET releases longitudinal analysis can use local archive paths explicit release dates. onet_panel_reconcile() classifies release--release changes real updates, stable resampling, stale carryforwards, recode/recalculation flags. onet_releases() lists O*NET database releases, archive URLs, dictionary URLs, release dates, inferred SOC taxonomy vintages.

title: onet2r 0.1.0
text: onet_cache_use(), onet_cache_clear(), onet_rate_limit() add optional local response caching request spacing repeated bulk O*NET API workflows. onet_join_oews() joins O*NET occupation tibbles national BLS Occupational Employment Wage Statistics (OEWS) employment wage estimates. onet_oews(), onet_oews_national(), onet_oews_state(), onet_oews_metro(), onet_oews_industry() download parse BLS OEWS estimates SOC-level wage employment context. onet_pums_employment_weights() converts ACS/CPS-style PUMS occupation records SOC-level employment weights O*NET joins. onet_weighted_summary() computes employment- wage-weighted summaries O*NET task, skill, ability, work-activity rows. Initial development release O*NET authentication, occupation search, occupation detail helpers, database table access, pagination, military crosswalks, taxonomy mapping.
